entry.title: [DDP] Iran Closed the Strait of Hormuz — Marandi Explains Why the US Is Losing
entry.summary:
Iran says the United States violated its agreement. Now the Strait of Hormuz is closed — and Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi explains what happens next. Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi joins Deep Dive Perspective to examine the Iran–US war, the collapse of the memorandum of understanding, and the strategic consequences of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. In Marandi’s account, the United States violated the agreement through renewed sanctions, threats, attacks, and attempts to undermine Iran’s authority in the Strait. He argues that Iran anticipated a renewed confrontation and used the ceasefire period to export oil, import essential supplies, expand underground facilities, and prepare its missile and drone capabilities.
The discussion also examines the depletion of US missile stocks, the difficulty of sustaining a prolonged war, the possibility of a ground offensive, the vulnerability of American bases and Gulf infrastructure, and the potential effect on global energy markets.
Marandi further argues that the conflict has strengthened Iran’s regional position and accelerated wider geopolitical changes, including closer cooperation between Iran, Russia, China, Yemen, Lebanon, and Iraq. He also discusses de-dollarisation, the future of the Middle East, and what he describes as the weakening of US regional hegemony.
These are the views and assessments expressed by the guest during the interview. They are presented for political analysis, public debate, and critical scrutiny.
🕒 Timestamps00:00 Introduction and the central question 01:10 Trump, ego, hubris, and the influence of political interests 02:29 The alleged violations of the Iran memorandum of understanding 03:02 Sanctions, threats, and pressure on Iran 03:20 The Strait of Hormuz clause 03:59 Ships, Iranian naval orders, and the escalation 05:23 The Strait of Hormuz is declared closed 05:37 Trump’s threats and Western political silence 07:07 What is the US trying to achieve? 07:44 US missile depletion and the limits of a prolonged war 08:54 Iran’s underground bases, missiles, and drones 10:09 The strategic consequences of the Oman mistake 11:20 Why Hormuz is central to the conflict 11:55 The possibility of a US ground offensive 12:46 Trump’s threats against Iranian infrastructure 14:02 Iran’s stated response to attacks on critical infrastructure 16:42 Threats against Gulf infrastructure and the risk of wider escalation 19:16 Could the US launch a ground invasion? 22:00 What has the US achieved, according to Marandi? 30:56 Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and Iran’s regional allies 33:25 Iran as a fourth centre of regional power 35:54 De-dollarisation and Turkey’s role 37:51 Closing remarks and the future of the conflict
👤 About Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi Dr. Said Mohammed Marandi is a professor and geopolitical commentator who provides analysis on Iran, US foreign policy, the Middle East, regional security, and the changing international order. In this interview, Marandi discusses the Iran–US conflict, the Strait of Hormuz, military strategy, economic pressure, the Axis of Resistance, and the wider consequences of the war. The views expressed are his own.
#Iran #StraitOfHormuz #IranUSWar #Geopolitics #SaidMohammedMarandi
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview belong to the individual guest and are presented for political commentary, analysis, and public debate. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse every position expressed. Viewers are encouraged to consult multiple sources and form their own conclusions.

entry.title: [DDP] America Has No Military Option: Ex-CIA Analyst on the Iran Crisis
entry.summary:
The United States provoked a war with Iran it cannot win. That is not a prediction — says former CIA analyst Larry C. Johnson. It is the current reality.
Former CIA analyst and State Department counter-terrorism official Larry C. Johnson returns to Deep Dive Perspective for what may be his most direct assessment yet. In Johnson's analysis, the United States entered a confrontation with Iran without the supply chain, the missile stocks, or the ground force capacity to win it — and is now facing the strategic consequences. Iran, he argues, has spent decades preparing for exactly this moment: investing in ballistic missiles, nuclear-attack-resistant shelters, and — most critically — consolidating control over the Strait of Hormuz, through which 40% of the world's usable oil passes. The result, in Johnson's assessment, is that Iran now holds what he calls "a nuclear option without nuclear weapons." On the US political system, Johnson is equally direct: a Congress he argues is incapable of acting as a check on the executive due to lobby dependency, and a president he says is "living in a delusional world" fed a fixed narrative about Iran that contradicts the intelligence. On the global economic consequences — shortages of helium, LNG, urea, and fertilizer, rising diesel and aviation fuel prices, and an inflationary spike Johnson attributes to genuine physical scarcity rather than monetary policy — he argues the world is witnessing something larger: what he calls "the death throes of the US hegemonic empire." These are Johnson's own analytical positions, presented here for debate and critical scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Johnson's core thesis: why he argues the US "lacks the supply chain and the military capability" to win a war with Iran • The Strait of Hormuz: 40% of global usable oil — and why Johnson calls Iranian control of it a "nuclear option without nuclear weapons" • Iran's missile stocks, nuclear-resistant underground shelters, and what Johnson says this means for any US ground invasion scenario • Saudi Arabia's role: why Johnson argues it is "committing suicide" by participating in US-led operations against the Houthis • Trump's "delusional world": Johnson's charge that the president is "fed a narrative from Israel" that contradicts the operational reality • Congress and lobby dependency: Johnson's argument that the legislative check on executive war-making has been neutralised — his view, presented for debate • The global economic cascade: helium shortages impacting chip production, fertilizer shortfalls threatening Southern Hemisphere harvests, and why Johnson says this inflation is structural — not monetary • De-dollarization accelerating: China's CIPS system vs SWIFT, countries selling US treasuries and buying gold, BRICS financial architecture
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction & Larry Johnson's Core Thesis 02:30 Iran's Ballistic Missiles and Nuclear-Resistant Shelters 05:00 The Strait of Hormuz — Iran Controls 40% of Global Oil 08:00 Saudi Arabia "Committing Suicide" — The Yambu Risk 10:00 Houthis and the Bab al-Mandab Threat 11:00 Trump's "Delusional World" — The Strait Is Not Open 14:00 US Military Deficiencies: Supply Chain and Ground Forces 18:00 Depleted Missile Stocks and the Rare Earth Problem 22:00 Iran's Nuclear Capacity — and the Fatwa Under Pressure 26:00 The China-Russia-Iran Security Treaty (Signed Jan 28) 30:00 US Air Bases Hit — Jordan, Diego Garcia 34:00 Congress and the Lobby Dependency — Johnson's Assessment 38:00 Israel as "Pariah State" — US Losing the Narrative 42:00 De-dollarization: CIPS, BRICS, and the End of the Petrodollar 46:00 "Death Throes of the US Hegemonic Empire" — Johnson's Final Assessment
👤 About Larry C. Johnson: Larry C. Johnson is a former analyst at the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and former deputy director at the US State Department's Office of Counter Terrorism. He served with the CIA from the 1980s and has since become a prominent independent commentator on US foreign policy, intelligence analysis, and West Asian geopolitics. He is a frequent critic of US military adventurism and has been an early commentator on both the Russia-Ukraine conflict and the US-Iran escalation. The views expressed in this interview are his own.
#LarryJohnson #USIranWar #StraitOfHormuz #Geopolitics #CIA
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analysis, and public debate. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse the positions stated. Viewers are encouraged to consult a wide range of sources and form their own conclusions. Nothing in this video constitutes an attack on any religious, ethnic, or national group.

entry.title: [DDP] Iran's TRUMP CARD: Why Closing The Strait Of Hormuz Would End The US Economy
entry.summary:
Dimitri Lascaris was in the streets of Tehran. He watched tens of millions gather and says the Western narrative about Iran is, in his words, "complete and utter bollocks." So what did the funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei really mean — and what happens next?
Independent journalist and podcast host Dimitri Lascaris returns to Deep Dive Perspective with an eyewitness account from the funeral events of Ayatollah Khamenei — joined by writer and analyst Raza John Vedadi. Together they examine what the staggering turnout of an estimated 40-43 million people across Tehran, Qom, Mashhad, Najaf, and Karbala actually tells us about the state of Iranian society, the limits of US coercive strategy, and where the conflict goes from here. Lascaris describes not just the scale, but the emotional tenor on the streets — mass indignation directed at Trump and, significantly, at Iranian government negotiators pursuing dialogue with what Lascaris calls "the treacherous Trump regime." Vedadi argues that the funeral demonstrated something more fundamental: that the Islamic Republic is institutionalised, not personality-dependent, and that Khamenei's death has, in his assessment, reinvigorated rather than weakened Iran's revolutionary spirit. Both guests examine Iran's strategic "trump card" — the Strait of Hormuz — and the broader question of whether China and Russia are missing a historic window to establish genuine deterrence against US unilateralism. These are the guests' own analytical assessments, presented here for debate and scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Dimitri Lascaris's eyewitness account from Tehran — what he saw, what the crowds felt, and why he calls Western coverage "bollocks" • Mass indignation on the streets: calls for Trump's assassination, hostility toward Iranian negotiators — Lascaris's direct account • Why Vedadi argues the funeral has "nurtured the tree of revolution in Iran once again" — his analytical framework • Trump's fundamental miscalculation on Iran: the assumption of popular hatred for Khamenei vs what Lascaris witnessed • Iran's strategic trump card: closing the Strait of Hormuz — 40% of usable global oil, plus Bab el-Mandeb and Suez • Why the US "would rather destroy its own economy than accept Iran as an equal partner" — Vedadi's argument • China and Russia: Lascaris expresses concern at their restraint and argues they are missing a "moment of opportunity" — his view, for analytical debate • Iran's growing self-sufficiency vs Gulf state dependency on external military guarantees • The role of independent media in dismantling the controlled Western narrative on Iran
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 01:30 Dimitri Lascaris: Eyewitness from Tehran — What He Saw 04:15 The Scale: Were There Really 40-43 Million People? 07:00 Mass Indignation — Anger at Trump AND at Iranian Negotiators 10:20 Iranian Woman's Message to the West: "This Is The Real Iran" 12:45 Trump's Fundamental Miscalculation Exposed 15:30 US "Plan B" — The Syrian Option Applied to Iran 18:00 Vedadi: "Khamenei's Blood Has Nurtured the Tree of Revolution" 21:10 Iran's Institutionalised State — Not Reliant on One Leader 24:30 Iran's TRUMP CARD: Closing the Strait of Hormuz 28:00 Bab el-Mandeb and Suez — The Full Scenario 31:15 Why the US Refuses to Treat Iran as an Equal — Vedadi's Analysis 35:00 China and Russia: Why Aren't They Doing More? 40:00 Iran's Allies: Political Cover in the UN Security Council 43:30 The Western Narrative's Fatal Flaw — and Independent Media's Role 47:00 Final Assessment: Where Does This Go From Here?
👤 About the Guests:
Dimitri Lascaris is a Canadian lawyer, journalist, and host of the podcast "Reason to Resist." A widely cited independent voice on international affairs and social justice, he has reported from conflict zones and political flashpoints internationally. He was present in Iran during the Khamenei funeral events.
Raza John Vedadi is a writer, analyst, and blogger covering Iranian affairs, geopolitics, and the multipolar world order. He writes on Substack and other independent platforms and brings an Iranian-heritage perspective to the analysis of Western media narratives and Iran's strategic position.
#KhomeiniFuneral #Iran #DimitriLascaris #Geopolitics #MiddleEast
📢Disclaimer:
The views and opinions expressed in this video are those of the hosts and guests and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of Deep Dive Perspective. The content provided is for informational and entertainment purposes only and should not be construed as professional advice. We encourage viewers to do their own research and form their own opinions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [DDP] Farage Is NOT Anti-Establishment — Chris Williamson Exposes Britain's Political Lie
entry.summary:
Chris Williamson — former Labour MP, now one of Britain's most uncompromising independent voices — says Nigel Farage is not anti-establishment. He IS the establishment. And that's just where he starts.
Former Derby North MP and political analyst Chris Williamson returns to Deep Dive Perspective for a forensic dismantling of the British political consensus. In Williamson's analysis, the public's choice at the ballot box is largely illusory: Labour, Conservative, Liberal Democrats and Reform UK all sit inside the same neoliberal, pro-corporate, pro-war framework — differentiated only by style, not substance. Farage, he argues, is the clearest example: privately educated, a former City commodities broker, multi-millionaire, donor-dependent — a figure who performs anti-establishment rhetoric while embodying establishment interests. On Starmer, Williamson is equally direct: a leader who made ten specific commitments to win his own party's leadership and broke every one of them. On economic policy, Williamson advances a Modern Monetary Theory-informed argument that a currency-sovereign government can never run out of money — and that the "bond vigilante" narrative used to justify austerity is, in his view, a managed myth. These are Williamson's own analytical positions, presented here for debate and scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Williamson argues Farage is "a pillar of the establishment, if truth be told" — his evidence and reasoning • Starmer's ten broken leadership pledges — what they were and why Williamson says this defines the man • Reform UK as "Thatcherite 2.0": what that means for working-class voters in Williamson's assessment • Andy Burnham: the most credible Labour alternative — and why Williamson still has serious reservations about him • Modern Monetary Theory explained: why Williamson argues Britain "can never run out of money" and what that means for public services • The case for public ownership: utilities, infrastructure, manufacturing — and why Williamson draws parallels with China's economic model • The National Security Act (2023): Williamson's charge that it is being used to silence political dissent • Palestine Declassified losing studio access following a Home Office letter — and what Williamson says this signals • Why he argues yielding to bad-faith accusations is the road to political extinction — the lesson he draws from Labour under Corbyn
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 01:15 Why Williamson Left Labour — and What He's Doing Now 03:40 The Illusion of Choice: Why All Mainstream Parties Serve the Same Interests 06:55 Nigel Farage: "A Pillar of the Establishment, If Truth Be Told" 11:20 Reform UK as Thatcherite 2.0 — Worse for Working-Class Britain 14:45 Keir Starmer: The Ten Broken Pledges 17:30 Andy Burnham: Best of a Bad Bunch or Genuine Alternative? 22:10 Modern Monetary Theory: "Britain Can Never Run Out of Money" 27:45 The "Bond Vigilante" Myth and Who It Serves 31:20 The Case for Public Ownership — and the China Model 36:00 Immigration: Cause, Effect and the "Fear" Narrative 39:15 The National Security Act — A Tool for Silencing Dissent? 43:30 Palestine Declassified and the Home Office Letter 47:00 Why You Cannot Yield to Bad-Faith Attacks — The Corbyn Lesson 51:20 What a Genuine Political Alternative Actually Requires
👤 About Chris Williamson: Chris Williamson is a British political activist and former Labour MP for Derby North (2010–2015 and 2017–2019). A close ally of Jeremy Corbyn, he was suspended by the Labour Party in 2019 and subsequently resigned. He is co-host of the show Palestine Declassified and a regular commentator on British and geopolitical affairs from an anti-imperialist, anti-neoliberal perspective. The views expressed in this interview are his own.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
#ChrisWilliamson #UKPolitics #NigelFarage #KeirStarmer #ReformUK
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analysis, and public debate. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse the positions stated. Viewers are encouraged to consult a wide range of sources and form their own conclusions.
Deep Dive Perspective assumes no responsibility or liability for any errors or omissions in the content. All information is provided on an as-is basis. Viewer discretion is advised.

entry.title: [DDP] Is Anti-Zionism Enough? Rabbi Beck, Atzmon & Marouf in Unfiltered Debate
entry.summary:
Gilad Atzmon says anti-Zionism is failing Palestine. Rabbi Beck defends his movement's strategy. Laith Marouf issues a challenge to Jewish institutions. The debate nobody in the movement wants to have.
In one of Deep Dive Perspective's most debated episodes, three voices with sharply different analyses of the Palestine solidarity movement sit together to interrogate a central strategic question: is "anti-Zionism" — as a framing, a movement, and a political identity — sufficient to address what is happening to the Palestinian people? Gilad Atzmon argues that the movement has been structurally constrained by its own refusal to examine harder questions about the nature of the state it opposes — a position he roots in his reading of Israeli self-definition. Rabbi Beck of Neturei Karta argues that the most effective anti-Zionist work happens within Jewish communities, separating Jewish identity from Zionist ideology and demonstrating that opposition to Zionism is consistent with Jewish religious tradition. Laith Marouf offers a third position: that the moment for public-facing Jewish anti-Zionism has passed, and what is now required is a direct confrontation with Zionist control of Jewish institutions from within. These are the guests' own analytical frameworks, presented here for debate and critical scrutiny.
🔍 In This Episode: • Gilad Atzmon's argument that "anti-Zionism" obscures rather than confronts the core issue — his analytical position, presented for debate • Why Atzmon argues the Israel-Palestine movement has structurally deferred the hardest questions • Rabbi Beck: how the Neturei Karta approach works — and why he argues it demonstrates opposition to Zionism is a Jewish religious imperative, not antisemitism • Laith Marouf's challenge: why he argues Jewish anti-Zionists must move from public solidarity to internal institutional confrontation • The theological and historical split in Judaism over Zionism — from opposition to rabbinical acceptance post-1967 • Whether the small size of explicitly anti-Zionist Jewish communities undermines or amplifies their moral argument • The three guests' competing frameworks for what an effective Palestine solidarity movement now requires • Is the debate between these positions a distraction — or the most important conversation the movement is not having?
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction & Context 02:32 Gilad Atzmon: Why He Questions the "Anti-Zionism" Label 04:00 "Zigzagging Around the Core Issue" — Atzmon's Critique of Movement Strategy 06:55 Laith Marouf: "Palestinians Have Defended Judaism for 100 Years" 09:23 Is Jewish Anti-Zionism "Just Talk"? The Demand for Action 17:07 The Movement's Crisis: Where Three Perspectives Converge and Diverge 18:40 Marouf: "The Rebellion Must Come From Inside Jewish Institutions" 21:55 Atzmon on Anti-Zionist Jewish Movements: A Statistical Reality Check 23:35 How Zionism Gained Rabbinical Legitimacy After 1967 24:49 Rabbi Beck Responds: The Role of Neturei Karta in the Wider Movement 34:56 The Debate Intensifies: What Are Anti-Zionist Jews Actually Prepared to Do? 39:39 Atzmon's Final Assessment — and Where the Three Guests End Up
👤 About the Guests:
Gilad Atzmon is a British-Israeli jazz musician, author, and political commentator. His books include The Wandering Who? (2011) and Being in time (2017). He is one of the most controversial voices in debates about Zionism, Jewish identity, and Palestine solidarity. The positions expressed in this interview are his own.
Rabbi Yisroel Dovid Weiss is a prominent spokesperson for Neturei Karta International — an Orthodox Jewish movement that considers Zionism a violation of Jewish religious law. He has spoken at international forums and pro-Palestine events worldwide, arguing that opposition to Zionism is a religious obligation, not antisemitism.
Laith Marouf is a Lebanese-Palestinian media producer and political commentator based in Canada. He has been a vocal critic of Israeli policies and a commentator on media representation of the Palestinian cause.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
#AntiZionism #Palestine #GiladAtzmon #RabbiBeck #Geopolitics
📢Disclaimer:
⚠️ The views expressed in this interview are those of the individual guests and are presented solely for the purposes of political commentary, analytical debate, and the free exchange of ideas. Deep Dive Perspective does not endorse, adopt, or promote the positions of any guest. Viewers are strongly encouraged to consult a wide range of perspectives and reach their own conclusions. Nothing in this video constitutes an attack on any religious, ethnic, or national group.

entry.title: [DDP] The Arab World's Dirty Secret: Who Is Really Helping Israel Win?
entry.summary:
Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and Ishan Safar-Innajad argue Arab leaders — led by the UAE — are not just failing Palestine, but actively enabling the occupation. The evidence is damning.
In a wide-ranging and forensic two-guest conversation, Egyptian journalist Dr. Ali Al-Kabani and geopolitical analyst Ishan Safar-Innajad deliver a detailed indictment of Arab leadership's record on Palestine — focusing specifically on the United Arab Emirates. In their analysis, the Abraham Accords were not a peace initiative but a strategic realignment that placed Gulf regimes on the side of a US-Israeli project designed to contain the resistance axis, neutralise Palestinian agency, and divide the wider Muslim world along manufactured sectarian lines. Both guests argue that the UAE's simultaneous operations in Sudan and Yemen — running parallel to its normalisation with Israel — reveal a coherent regional strategy rather than a collection of isolated policy decisions. With Iran's strategic depth and self-sufficiency growing, they contend the Gulf states' dependency on external military guarantees is becoming an existential liability rather than a source of security.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Al-Kabani argues the UAE's normalisation with Israel makes it, in his words, "complicit" in events in Gaza — his view, presented for analytical debate • The UAE's alleged role in Sudan: destabilising the south to keep Egypt occupied on its own border • How the UAE is accused of operating a military facility in Yemen to monitor and constrain Ansar Allah movements • The gold mines of Sudan — and why guests argue UAE wealth extraction is directly tied to Sudanese instability • The Abraham Accords as a Gulf competition for US protection — not a peace process • Muslim Brotherhood as a regional proxy instrument — analysis of how guests assess its deployment across multiple conflicts • The Sunni-Shia divide: why both guests argue it is a manufactured colonial framework, not an organic religious schism • Iran vs the Gulf states: self-sufficiency, space industry, and strategic depth compared • What happens to Gulf regimes if the resistance axis is ever defeated — the guests' sobering assessment
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 00:09 Opening: Gaza as a Revelation — Arab Leaders Exposed 00:40 The UAE: Worst Case of Normalisation — Sudan & Israel 02:13 How Deep Is the Arab Betrayal of Palestine? 02:40 UAE Activity in Sudan, Aden & Yemen Drone Operations 06:50 The Resistance Axis vs UAE-Backed Proxy Groups 07:22 Is This Already a Regional War? The Guests' Assessment 08:33 Mohammed bin Zayed: Al-Kabani's Charges Against Abu Dhabi 09:00 The Abraham Accords — UAE as Broker for US-Israeli Project 12:38 UAE Airport on Socotra: Monitoring Ansar Allah 14:10 Sudan's Gold Mines & UAE Economic Extraction 17:50 The Money Trail: Washington, Tel Aviv & London 18:10 Gulf States' Competition for US Military Protection 19:35 Muslim Brotherhood: How Guests Assess Its Role as Proxy 32:15 Elections & the Brotherhood: The Democratic Paradox 35:00 The Sunni-Shia Divide as a Colonial Construct 37:31 Gulf Leaders' Disconnect from Strategic Reality 40:00 Iran's Strategic Depth vs Gulf State Dependency 42:00 The Resistance Axis as Existential Shield for All
👤 About the Guests:
Dr. Ali Al-Kabani is an Egyptian journalist and political analyst with extensive experience covering Arab affairs and Middle Eastern geopolitics.
Ishan Safar-Nijad is a geopolitical commentator and analyst with a focus on Middle Eastern power dynamics, the resistance axis, and the broader regional alignment of Arab states.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.

entry.title: [DDP] The UK's Political Psyop: Piers Corbyn on Farage, Starmer & The Hidden Agenda
entry.summary:
Piers Corbyn argues that Farage, Starmer and Reform are all playing the same game — and the real power pulling the strings has nothing to do with Westminster.
Political activist, former London Mayoral candidate and brother of Jeremy Corbyn, Piers Corbyn, joins Deep Dive Perspective for a wide-ranging and unfiltered assessment of Britain's political landscape. In Corbyn's analysis, the traditional left-right divide is now a managed performance: Labour has abandoned its base, Reform positions itself as a "dustbin of discontent" without coherent policy, and both, he argues, ultimately serve the same elite interests. He outlines a four-point programme he believes any credible leader must adopt, delivers a forensic critique of media bias and the BBC's role in narrative control, and explains why he believes corporate power — not Parliament — is the real locus of British political decision-making.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Corbyn says Labour "lost its way" — and what voters are actually telling him on the doorstep • His argument that Reform is being used as a "dustbin of discontent" to divide the opposition • The four-point programme Corbyn says every leader must commit to • Immigration: why he calls the boat crisis a deliberate "psychological operation" — his words • Media bias, the BBC, and who Corbyn believes the coverage is designed to protect • The smearing of the Green Party — and why he sees the same playbook used against his brother Jeremy • "Corbynomics" vs the mega-corporations: what the establishment fears most • Corporate accountability and whether the new BBC director can rehabilitate public broadcasting
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 00:09 Piers Corbyn: Physics, Astrophysics, Activism & Political History 02:03 Why Corbyn Despairs at Modern British Politics 04:27 Starmer, Farage & Reform — "All Part of the Same Game" 06:59 Corbyn's Four-Point Programme for Political Change 08:12 Immigration: "It's a Psychological Operation" — Corbyn's View 12:43 Media Bias, the WEF & Why Corbyn Believes Starmer Serves the Agenda 15:25 The Smearing of the Greens — Same Playbook as Jeremy Corbyn 18:48 "Corbynomics" vs Mega-Corporations & Corporate Power 19:48 Government Accountability, the BBC & What Comes Next
👤 About Piers Corbyn: Piers Richard Corbyn is a British political activist, astrophysicist, and long-range weather forecaster. Brother of former Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn, he has stood for Mayor of London, served as a Southwark councillor, and has been involved in anti-racist campaigns and housing activism since the 1970s. He is known for his outspoken critique of mainstream political consensus.
🌊 Deep Dive Perspective is an independent geopolitical channel featuring long-form conversations with journalists, analysts, academics, and independent thinkers on global power, international relations, media narratives, and the emerging multipolar world.
🔔 SUBSCRIBE for more uncensored analysis that connects the dots the establishment wants to hide: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0

entry.title: [DDP] Scott Ritter: Iran WON & Trump Knows It — The Hormuz MOU Is A Facade
entry.summary:
Military analyst Scott Ritter calls the US-Iran Strait of Hormuz MOU a "political facade" — and argues America under Trump is fundamentally "agreement incapable." What does that mean for the Middle East?
Former UN weapons inspector Scott Ritter delivers a forensic breakdown of the Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) signed between the United States and Iran on the Strait of Hormuz. According to Ritter, the agreement deliberately sidesteps every hard issue — Iran's nuclear programme, its ballistic missile capability, and its regional posture — deferring them to a 60-day consultation process designed, in his assessment, to hand Trump a domestic political win ahead of the midterms rather than produce a durable settlement. With Iran increasingly integrated into BRICS and the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation, Ritter argues the window for effective American economic leverage is closing fast — and may already be shut.
🔍 In This Episode: • Why Ritter characterises the Hormuz MOU as an "act of political desperation" • The three critical issues the agreement deliberately avoids • Why the Trump administration is, in Ritter's words, "agreement incapable" • How Iran's BRICS and SCO integration is rendering US sanctions increasingly ineffective • The strategic basis for Ritter's claim that "Iran won the war" • Israel's diminishing leverage and the prospect of a Trump–Netanyahu rupture • The collapse of the Abraham Accords and the "Sunni crescent" strategy • De-dollarisation, multipolarity, and the emerging post-American world order
🕒 Timestamps: 00:00 Introduction 00:09 The Hormuz MOU: Why Ritter Calls It a "Political Facade" 05:22 "The US Is Agreement Incapable" — Ritter's Core Verdict on Trump 06:22 Trump's Real Motive: Midterms, Oil Prices & Domestic Politics 09:31 "Iran Won the War" — A Strategic Assessment 10:39 BRICS Integration & The Collapse of US Sanctions Leverage 14:08 Israel's Diminishing Military and Diplomatic Power 15:22 A Coming Trump–Netanyahu Rupture? 17:24 Netanyahu's Strategic Bankruptcy — "His Ego Has Written Checks the Body Can't Cash" 19:17 The Collapse of the "Sunni Crescent" Strategy 20:22 The Abraham Accords: Dead and Buried 24:39 Multipolarity, De-dollarisation & the Emerging World Order

entry.title: [DDP] UK Surgeon Prosecuted for Criticising Israel — The Free Speech Crisis Exposed
entry.summary:
A British surgeon prosecuted, suspended & arrested — not for malpractice, but for criticising Israel. Dr Ranjeet Brar & Ryan D expose how the IHRA definition is being weaponised to criminalise dissent.
The IHRA definition of antisemitism was never about protecting Jewish people. According to guests Dr Ranjeet Brar— surgeon, Communist Party of Great Britain (ML) leader, and judicial review applicant — and independent analyst Ryan Dawson, it was designed to conflate a nation-state with an ethnic group, making criticism of Israel's policies legally and professionally dangerous.
Brar details being arrested three times at demonstrations, suspended from surgical duties, and facing two General Medical Council fitness-to-practice investigations — all for speaking against Israeli state violence. Dawson documents a systematic pattern of social media de-platforming, financial censorship, and academic suppression coordinated through organisations like the ADL and Canary Mission across NATO and Five Eyes countries.
This is not a debate about antisemitism. It's a forensic examination of how states weaponize the language of anti-racism to protect the commission of what both guests call genocide — and what happens to those who refuse to stay silent.
**In This Episode:** • Ranjeet Brar's three arrests, GMC investigations & surgical suspension • The origins of the IHRA definition — who created it and why • How the definition was rolled out across UK institutions, police, Labour & the NHS • Cenk Uygur banned from the UK and blocked from Oxford debate • Ryan D on being banned from PayPal, Patreon, YouTube, TikTok & more • Christian Zionism, Peter Thiel, Musk & Zuckerberg's ties to Zionist networks • Why the anti-Muslim hatred definition included free speech protections — and the IHRA didn't • The judicial review challenging IHRA's legality under Article 10 / Human Rights Act • Tommy Robinson, Nigel Farage & who is actually funding the far right • "If you don't have free speech, you can't fix anything downstream from that" • Why Brar argues: real antisemitism is being buried by false accusations
---
🕐 TIMESTAMPS
00:00 – Introduction: Ranjeet Brar & the Cost of Speaking Out 02:15 – What Is the IHRA Definition of Antisemitism? 05:30 – Origins: Who Created It and Why 09:00 – How It Was Rolled Out Across UK Institutions 13:20 – Ranjeet Brar: Arrested Three Times for Palestine Activism 17:45 – The GMC Investigations: "I'm Not Allowed to Save Lives if I Criticise Israel" 22:10 – Cenk Uygur Banned from the UK: A Case Study 25:40 – Ryan D: Banned from Every Platform — Canary Mission & the ADL 31:00 – The Nation vs Ethnicity Confusion: A Simple Trick 35:20 – Zionism Is Not Judaism — A Critical Distinction 39:50 – Who Funds the Far Right? Tommy Robinson, Nigel Farage & Israeli Networks 45:00 – The Judicial Review: Challenging IHRA Under the Human Rights Act 50:30 – Christian Zionism, Big Tech & the Architecture of Censorship 55:00 – "If You Don't Have Free Speech, You Can't Fix Anything" — Closing Arguments
---
👤 GUESTS
**Ranjeet Brar** — Surgeon, Head of the Communist Party of Great Britain (Marxist-Leninist) Ranjeet Brar is a practising NHS surgeon who has been arrested three times at pro-Palestine demonstrations, suspended from his surgical duties, and subjected to two GMC fitness-to-practice investigations — all stemming from his vocal criticism of Israeli state policy. He is currently pursuing a judicial review to challenge the legality of the IHRA definition under Article 10 of the European Convention on Human Rights and the UK Human Rights Act.
**Ryan D** — Independent Geopolitical Analyst & Commentator Returning Deep Dive Perspective guest and one of the most extensively censored independent analysts online. Ryan has been banned from PayPal, Patreon, YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Discord, Coinbase, Streamlabs, Google Ads, and TikTok, largely through pressure from organisations like the ADL. He holds what he describes as "the longest Canary Mission rap sheet" and continues to produce forensic geopolitical analysis outside mainstream platform control.
---
🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ

entry.title: [DDP] Israel Is Collapsing From Within — Jewish Anti-Zionist Exposes Why
entry.summary:
A Jewish anti-Zionist says Israel is dismantling itself from within — and the evidence is mounting. With Sam Weinstein (IJAN) & Ryan Dawson, we name what mainstream media won't.
Israel's military campaigns across West Asia — Lebanon, Gaza, Iran — aren't projecting strength. They're accelerating a collapse no Western outlet will acknowledge. Sam Weinstein, Coordinator at the International Jewish Anti-Zionist Network (IJAN), argues that every bomb dropped is another nail in Zionism's coffin.
Ryan D. examines the geopolitical mechanics: Iran's red lines, the JCPOA failures, and a US foreign policy vacuum now filled by — in Dawson's words — "two real estate agents from New York."This is not a debate about whether Israel will face accountability. It's a forensic examination of how, when, and from which direction the reckoning is already arriving.
In This Episode: • Iran's red lines violated — and the military responses that followed • Trump's "knock it off" diplomacy and why it's failing in real-time • Sam Weinstein on IJAN's call to dismantle the state of Israel • Why there are more Christian Zionists in the US than Jews in the world • The Strait of Hormuz, Red Sea disruption, and global energy consequences • How social media and TikTok have irreversibly shifted youth opinion • Internal Israeli dissent: draft refusal, BDS, and generational rupture • The replacement of real US diplomats with political amateurs • Iran's resistance economy and asymmetric strategic depth • IJAN's campaign against the Jewish National Fund's charitable status • "Never Again for Anyone" — the Jewish tradition of standing with the oppressed

entry.title: [DDP] The Next Middle East War May Already Have Started
entry.summary:
The conflict involving Israel, Lebanon and Iran continues to raise questions about the possibility of a wider regional confrontation.
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, journalist and media analyst Laith Marouf joins us to discuss developments on the Israel–Lebanon front, the role of Hezbollah, the strategic calculations of Iran, and the broader implications for regional stability.
We explore military developments, political narratives, international diplomacy, the role of the United States, and what a prolonged conflict could mean for the future of the Middle East.
Whether you agree or disagree with the perspectives discussed, this conversation aims to examine the forces shaping one of the most consequential geopolitical crises of our time.
📌 Chapters: 00:00 Introduction 00:52 Why Israel Keeps Returning to Lebanon 01:59 Battlefield Reality vs Political Narrative 04:43 The Israeli Military and the Question of Morale 09:52 Escalation and Civilian Displacement 11:42 The Wider Regional Stakes 18:11 Iran, Hezbollah and Ceasefire Dynamics 23:59 Global Power and Political Influence 28:32 The Future Beyond the Conflict 32:29 Democracy, Power and Political Systems 38:32 The Future of the Resistance Axis 46:20 Closing Thoughts
About the Guest: Laith Marouf is a Canadian journalist, media consultant and political commentator. He founded Free Palestine TV in 2023 and has worked extensively in community media, communications strategy and international affairs. He currently serves as a Senior Consultant at the Community Media Advocacy Centre and regularly provides analysis on Middle East politics, media narratives and geopolitical developments.

entry.content:
The conflict involving Israel, Lebanon and Iran continues to raise questions about the possibility of a wider regional confrontation.

In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, journalist and media analyst Laith Marouf joins us to discuss developments on the Israel–Lebanon front, the role of Hezbollah, the strategic calculations of Iran, and the broader implications for regional stability.

We explore military developments, political narratives, international diplomacy, the role of the United States, and what a prolonged conflict could mean for the future of the Middle East.

Whether you agree or disagree with the perspectives discussed, this conversation aims to examine the forces shaping one of the most consequential geopolitical crises of our time.

📌 Chapters:
00:00 Introduction
00:52 Why Israel Keeps Returning to Lebanon
01:59 Battlefield Reality vs Political Narrative
04:43 The Israeli Military and the Question of Morale
09:52 Escalation and Civilian Displacement
11:42 The Wider Regional Stakes
18:11 Iran, Hezbollah and Ceasefire Dynamics
23:59 Global Power and Political Influence
28:32 The Future Beyond the Conflict
32:29 Democracy, Power and Political Systems
38:32 The Future of the Resistance Axis
46:20 Closing Thoughts

About the Guest:
Laith Marouf is a Canadian journalist, media consultant and political commentator. He founded Free Palestine TV in 2023 and has worked extensively in community media, communications strategy and international affairs. He currently serves as a Senior Consultant at the Community Media Advocacy Centre and regularly provides analysis on Middle East politics, media narratives and geopolitical developments.

🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ

entry.title: [DDP] Ken Loach: Why the Media Still Gets Palestine Wrong
entry.summary:
Why does coverage of Palestine remain one of the most contested issues in modern journalism? Ken Loach joins Deep Dive Perspective to discuss media narratives, international law, and public opinion.
Award-winning filmmaker and long-time Palestine advocate Ken Loach joins Deep Dive Perspective for a wide-ranging discussion on media coverage of Palestine, international law, public opinion, and the role of Western political institutions.
🔍The conversation explores:
✔️Media narratives around Palestine ✔️Reporting on Gaza ✔️Language and framing in journalism ✔️International law and accountability ✔️The role of the BBC and Western media ✔️Political responses in the UK ✔️The future of international institutions ✔️Public opinion and social media
Guests Info: Ken Loach is one of Britain's most acclaimed filmmakers, known for award-winning films including Kes, I, Daniel Blake, and The Wind That Shakes the Barley. Throughout his career, he has combined filmmaking with political activism, becoming one of the UK's most prominent voices on social justice, workers' rights, anti-war movements, and Palestinian solidarity.

entry.title: [DDP] Inside Iran: What the West Still Doesn’t Understand About This War
entry.summary:
Iran and the United States are moving deeper into one of the most dangerous geopolitical confrontations in decades — but how is the conflict being viewed from inside Iran itself?
In this episode of Deep Dive Perspective, geopolitical analyst Rayan D. and Dr. Setareh Sadeqi of the University of Tehran discuss the Iran–US crisis, media narratives, propaganda warfare, sanctions, regional escalation, and how ordinary Iranians are reacting to growing tensions with Washington and Israel.
GUEST INFO: Rayan D.: Geopolitical analyst and author Dr. Setareh Sadeqi: Assistant Professor at the University of Tehran's Faculty of World Studies
🔔 Subscribe for long-form geopolitical analysis and independent conversations on global affairs: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
📌 WATCH NOW: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEq0jQ5NLgh_-RltiA-LSRCt https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEoqX56Fu2UbcO5_cEXf6tIC https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLLeWYbRjgFEqd42sdRwAXhuQbbGY22Bn0
The conversation explores:
US–Iran relations ✔️information warfare and propaganda ✔️media narratives around Iran ✔️the role of social media and cultural influence ✔️sanctions and their impact on Iranian society ✔️generational shifts inside Iran ✔️geopolitics of the Middle East ✔️ideology, resistance, and international power
Rather than headlines and soundbites, this discussion focuses on long-form analysis, historical context, and perspectives rarely heard in Western media coverage.

entry.title: [DDP] America Spent Decades Trying to Stop Iran. It Didn't Work. Patrick Henningsen
entry.summary:
In this episode, Patrick Henningsen joins Deep Dive Perspective to examine the escalating confrontation between the United States, Israel, and Iran — and why the geopolitical consequences may reshape the Middle East for years to come.
The discussion explores the Strait of Hormuz crisis, economic fallout, regional power shifts, sanctions, oil supply disruption, and the growing debate over whether American influence in the region is weakening.
🔍 Topics covered include:
✔️US–Iran tensions ✔️The Strait of Hormuz ✔️Middle East geopolitics ✔️Energy and global markets ✔️Israel and regional escalation ✔️Sanctions and economic warfare ✔️The future of US power in the region

entry.title: [DDP] What Syria Can Teach Us About Iran — Dr. Piers Robinson
entry.summary:
They said Syria used chemical weapons. The OPCW's own scientists now say the evidence was manipulated. They're making the same claims about Iran. Dr. Piers Robinson has spent years documenting exactly how this is done.
A researcher who works alongside former OPCW Director-General Jose Bustani — the man John Bolton had removed from his post before the Iraq invasion — Robinson lays out a forensic case that the same institutional machinery used to justify the Syria intervention has been reactivated against Iran. The OPCW's Douma and Ghouta investigations were contested from the inside: internal whistleblowers leaked documents to WikiLeaks, testified publicly in Brussels in 2019, and were corroborated by mainstream journalists including Peter Hitchens. The official story didn't hold — and almost nobody covered it.
Now the IAEA is making assessments about Iran's nuclear programme. The UN is providing the political framework. The same playbook. The same institutions. The same pattern — one episode earlier in the timeline.
🔍 In This Episode: ✔️The OPCW: what it was built to do — and what it became ✔️Jose Bustani removed by John Bolton before Iraq — and what that tells you about institutional independence ✔️Ghouta 2013 and Douma 2018: what the OPCW whistleblowers actually said ✔️The White Helmets — UK-government-linked intelligence on the ground in Syria ✔️The WikiLeaks OPCW documents and the 2019 Brussels testimony nobody reported ✔️The IAEA's double standard: Iran's alleged programme vs. Israel's undeclared nuclear arsenal ✔️The UN's structural inability to restrain the most powerful states — and its resemblance to the League of Nations ✔️Wesley Clark's "seven countries in five years" — and where Iran fits in that sequence ✔️Whether these institutions can be reformed — or whether the BRICS/Eurasian alternative architecture is the only viable path ✔️What ordinary people can do when the watchdogs have become attack dogs
🎙️ Guest: Dr. Piers Robinson — Academic Researcher & Author (writing on OPCW investigations in Syria; collaborator with Hans von Sponik & Jose Bustani). Expert specializing in propaganda, international relations, global conflicts, and the integrity of international oversight institutions.
In this episode, Dr. Piers Robinson joins us to discuss chemical weapons, propaganda, and the ongoing push for regime change. We explore how these factors shape international relations, particularly concerning Iran and the broader Middle East, offering a deep dive into foreign policy.

entry.title: [DDP] I Spent 11 Days Inside Iran During the War — Here's What They're Not Telling You
entry.summary:
He crossed into Iran overland from Turkey. Spent 11 days on the ground. Visited 7 cities. Boarded a civilian vessel into the Strait of Hormuz. Journalist and lawyer Dimitri Lascaris went inside the country Western media calls America's greatest enemy — and what he found contradicts almost everything you've been told.
Lascaris — who has reported from conflict zones across the globe — returned from Iran with a forensic account of a nation that is far more sophisticated, more resilient, and more united than any Western outlet will admit. In this episode he breaks down the real motivation behind US aggression against Iran (it's not the Israel lobby alone — it's a 75-year strategy for global domination and control of West Asia's resources), the catastrophic economic consequences already in motion if the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted, and why Trump has failed every single stated objective of this war — while a "Round Two" may already be in preparation.
🔍 In This Episode: ✔️Dimitri's 11-day journey: 7 cities, the Strait of Hormuz & crossing into Iran by land from Turkey ✔️Why Iran is "far more sophisticated, resilient and united" than Western media portrays ✔️The real US motivation: global domination, strategic resources & the Wesley Clark "7 countries" plan ✔️Why Dimitri rejects the "Israel lobby as sole driver" narrative — and what the real driver is ✔️Economic catastrophe: economists predict famine within 3–5 months if Hormuz stays disrupted ✔️"September is the deadline" — the supply chain timeline the West isn't discussing ✔️Three US carrier battle groups in the region: preparation for Round Two? ✔️Trump's failed objectives: regime change, insurrection, decapitation, missile destruction ✔️How the war has united Iranians "to an extraordinary degree" — what Dimitri witnessed on the streets ✔️Sanctions as economic terrorism: the real cost to Iranian civilians over decades ✔️What Dimitri actually saw in Iran: infrastructure, food supply, homelessness, women, democracy ✔️The Minnaab strike: 168 students and teachers killed on the first day of the war ✔️Is a deliberate global economic crash part of a larger strategy? Dimitri's assessment ✔️What meaningful resistance looks like — and what ordinary people can actually do

entry.title: [DDP] The Iran Conflict Is No Longer Staying Overseas
entry.summary:
A British-Iranian citizen was stabbed outside Downing Street at a peaceful protest. Who allowed it to happen — and why does it trace back to Britain's foreign policy on Iran?
When Mohammed Raza attended a peaceful rally in London, he was attacked from behind with knives. His attackers, allegedly linked to pro-Pahlavi organisations operating freely on British soil, had previously threatened his four-year-old daughter. The police were slow to respond. The ambulance was delayed. And the organisations allegedly responsible continue to operate with impunity.
This is blowback — and it is happening on our own streets.
Deep Dive Perspective brings together Hos Shafi (Workers Party Leadership Council), Nili Brook (journalist and founder of Iran's Resistance Rising), Raza Qasim (Chief Steward, Islamic Human Rights Commission), and Dr. Marzia Ansari (lawyer) to examine the foreign policy decisions and domestic failures that created the conditions for this attack — and what it reveals about who is really controlling British political space.
The views expressed in this interview are those of the guests and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🔪 The Downing Street stabbing: what happened to Mohammed Raza, the police response, and why the attackers may have been operating a planned operation 🚔 Why British police treat pro-Palestinian and pro-Iranian protesters through a lens of systemic bias — while violent counter-groups operate freely 📺 Iran International: its alleged foreign funding, its role in inciting violence against British-Iranians, and why the British government refuses to act ☠️ The MKO (Mujahedin-e Khalq): once a proscribed terrorist organisation, now attending UK parliamentary events — how did that happen? 🏛️ How foreign influence is shaping British domestic policy — and what Hussain Shafiei means by Britain being "occupied by a foreign power" ⚖️ Dr. Marzia Ansari on the legal case for proscribing dangerous groups operating on British soil 🌍 The manipulation of the asylum system: how asylum claims are being manufactured by organisations inciting violence against the Iranian state
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it challenged your understanding of what's happening on British streets. 💬 Comment below — should Iran International be investigated and held to account in the UK?
Guests Info: Hussain Shafiei: Member of the Workers Party Leadership Council. Nilly Brook: Independent journalist and founder of Iran's Resistance Rising. Reza Kazim: Chief Steward and representative from the Islamic Human Rights Commission. Dr. Marzieh Ansari: Legal professional and expert in international and domestic law.

entry.title: [DDP] Why Europe Still Can't Act Independently | Mick Wallace
entry.summary:
"Europe is the loyal tail that wags every time America barks." Former EU MP Mick Wallace and journalist Myriam Charabaty expose the foreign policy scandal Europe's leaders won't admit.
Europe presents itself to the world as a defender of human rights, democracy, and international law. But as the war on Gaza enters its second year and NATO's grip on European decision-making tightens, a very different picture emerges. Deep Dive Perspective sits down with former EU Member of Parliament Mick Wallace and Beirut-based journalist Myriam Charabaty to dissect why Europe cannot — or will not — act independently of Washington on the most consequential geopolitical questions of our time.
From the economics of energy dependency to the military intelligence role European nations play in West Asia, this is a forensic examination of the gap between what European leaders say and what they actually do.The views expressed in this interview are those of the guests and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
Guests Info: Mick Wallace: Former Member of the European Parliament (MEP) and outspoken political commentator on global macroeconomic affairs. Myriam Charabaty: Independent geopolitical journalist and analyst based in Beirut, Lebanon.
In This Episode: 🐕 Why Europe has become a "loyal tail" to US foreign policy — and what NATO's true function really is 💰 How following US foreign policy is costing European economies billions — the LNG price scandal ✈️ The secret military role of European nations (France, UK, Germany) in the West Asia conflict 🇺🇳 Why the EU still refuses to condemn Israel despite the deaths of over 20,000 children in Gaza 🇸🇾 Europe's role in the destabilisation of Syria — and what it reveals about Western "values" 🗳️ Is European democracy failing? The disconnect between public opinion and elite foreign policy 🌍 What Mick Wallace's advice to ordinary European citizens actually means — and why it matters
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines.
👍 Like this video if it challenged your view of European foreign policy.
💬 Comment below — do you think Europe will ever achieve genuine foreign policy independence?

entry.title: [DDP] The Iran Ceasefire Is More Fragile Than It Looks | Deep Dive
entry.summary:
Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire is already expiring. And according to Ehsan Safar Najad, Iran hasn't even played its full deck of cards yet.
As the dust settles on the Iran-US-Israel semi-stalemate, the critical question isn't whether there was a ceasefire — it's whether it means anything at all. Deep Dive Perspective sits down with independent analyst and host of The Political Aficionado, Ehsan Safar Najad, to break down Iran's strategic position in the aftermath of conflict: from its stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz, to the 10-point negotiating framework the US refuses to accept, to the nuclear deterrence calculation that has Washington and Tel Aviv at a standstill.
The ceasefire, as Ehsan puts it, "has an expiration date like a carton of milk left outside during July."
The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🚢 Why the Strait of Hormuz remains Iran's most powerful strategic lever — and why the US knows it 🤝 Iran's 10-point peace framework vs. the US's 15-point maximalist demands — why no deal is likely ☢️ Iran's nuclear deterrence calculus: the North Korea comparison and what 460kg of enriched uranium means 💰 How Iran is maintaining oil revenues at 2.3 million barrels/day despite Western sanctions 🌍 The global economic consequences: 9 strategic commodities, fertiliser shortages, and the famine horizon ⚔️ Why the next round of conflict will be "even more devastating" — and what Iran's unused cards look like
🔔 Subscribe to Deep Dive Perspective for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it changed how you see the Iran-US standoff. 💬 Comment below — does this ceasefire hold, or are we heading for round two?

entry.title: [DDP] How a Smaller Force Keeps Challenging Israel | Laith Marouf
entry.summary:
3,000 Hezbollah fighters. 120,000 Israeli soldiers. Two years of war — and Israel hasn't captured a single village. Laith Marouf breaks down who's really winning in West Asia.
As Western media scrambles to explain the military stalemate in Southern Lebanon, broadcaster and analyst Laith Marouf — host of Free Palestine TV — joins Deep Dive Perspective to cut through the propaganda and assess the real balance of forces on the ground. From the strategic depth of Lebanon's terrain, to Iran's calculated acceptance of a ceasefire, to the slow collapse of Israel's military mythology in the age of citizen journalism — Marouf delivers a forensic, unfiltered reading of where this 100-year conflict is heading.The views expressed in this interview are those of the guest and are presented here for analytical and educational purposes. Deep Dive Perspective does not necessarily endorse all positions stated.
In This Episode: 🪖 Why 3,000 Hezbollah fighters have stalled 120,000 Israeli soldiers in Southern Lebanon 🤝 The strategic logic behind Iran's ceasefire acceptance — weakness or calculation? 📱 How citizen journalism is dismantling decades of Western and Zionist propaganda 👑 The role of Arab monarchies in sustaining Western influence in the region ⚔️ Whether the Axis of Resistance can deliver a decisive military outcome 🗺️ The "100-year war" framework — and whether the next chapter has already begun
🔔 Subscribe for geopolitical analysis that goes beyond the headlines. 👍 Like this video if it challenged your understanding of the conflict.
💬 Comment below — we read every response.

entry.title: [DDP] The Epstein Files Exposed: Transhumanism, Media Control & the World They're Building Without You
entry.summary:
The Epstein files are finally in the public domain. But two investigators argue the real story goes far deeper than one man, one island, or one network.
Tony Gosling — investigative journalist and former BBC broadcaster — and Ryan Dawson — geopolitical analyst and author — trace the architecture of what they call the "Epstein class": an interconnected network of power that spans media institutions, technology corporations, financial systems, and political structures. Their argument is not about one individual but about a systemic, generational project — one designed, they claim, to engineer a techno-feudalist future in which wealth consolidates upward, institutions are deliberately corrupted, and the public is reduced to passive consumers of a pre-designed reality.
From Yuval Harari's "hacking humans" philosophy to the World Economic Forum's "own nothing, be happy" agenda; from the acquisition of food systems and land to the weaponisation of AI — Gosling and Dawson map what they see as a convergent, coordinated strategy of control, and ask: what does meaningful resistance actually look like?
🔍 In This Episode:
✔️What is the "Epstein class" — and why does the term matter beyond Epstein himself? ✔️The historical roots of the techno-feudalist agenda: eugenics, social Darwinism and the 1930s ✔️Yuval Harari and the philosophy of "hacking humans" — inevitable evolution or deliberate agenda? ✔️How the "Epstein class" profits from major global crises: the pattern across decades ✔️Media monopoly and institutional capture: how the "wrong people" are placed in positions of power ✔️The WEF "own nothing" agenda and the acquisition of land, food systems and resources ✔️Peter Thiel, Palantir, and the digitalisation of surveillance — the Eye of Sauron becomes real ✔️AI, privacy and the collapse of consent ✔️Elon Musk's grandfather and the historical roots of technocratic ideology ✔️The role of alternative media, boycotts and moral re-armament in building genuine resistance ✔️Why withdrawing consent — from products, platforms and ideology — is the most powerful act available ✔️What virtue ethics across traditions (Taoism, Islam, Christianity, Bushido) offer as a counter-framework
🎙️ Guests: Tony Gosling — Investigative Journalist & Former BBC Broadcaster Ryan Dawson — Geopolitical Analyst & Author
Timestamps and Highlights: 0:00 Introduction — What Is the "Epstein Class"? 3:00 Beyond Epstein: The Network Behind the Name 7:00 Social Darwinism & the God Complex of the Ultra-Wealthy 12:00 Historical Roots: Eugenics, Theosophy & the 1930s Dream 17:00 Technocratic Ideology — From Bertrand Russell to the WEF 22:00 Yuval Harari: "Hacking Humans" & the Transhumanist Agenda 27:00 How the Epstein Class Profits From Global Crises 32:00 Media Monopoly & Institutional Capture — Staffing With the Wrong People 37:00 The Land Grab: Buying Up Food, Resources & Infrastructure 41:00 Peter Thiel, Palantir & the Architecture of Digital Surveillance 45:00 AI, Privacy & the Collapse of Consent 49:00 What Meaningful Resistance Actually Looks Like 53:00 Boycotts, Alternative Media & Withdrawing Your Consent 57:00 Virtue Ethics as a Counter-Framework — What Traditions Offer

entry.title: [DDP] Ex-IDF, Ex-Israeli: Gilad Atzmon on What's Really Driving Israel's Genocidal Logic
entry.summary:
Gilad Atzmon served in the IDF. He left Israel at 30. He now identifies as neither Israeli nor Jewish. And he argues that what's driving the war on Iran isn't Zionism — it's something older, deeper, and far more dangerous.
In this episode, Atzmon — international jazz musician, bestselling author and former IDF conscript — makes the case that Israel has undergone a fundamental transformation: from a secular Zionist project into a theocratic, religiously-driven state shaped by Judaic scripture, Rabbi Kook's philosophy, and a political culture of impunity. He traces this shift from the kibbutz era through Likud's 1977 victory, Netanyahu's 1996 electoral realignment, and into the religious nationalism of Ben-Gvir and Smotrich that now drives Israeli state policy — including the war on Iran.
🔍 In This Episode:
✔️Why Gilad Atzmon identifies as "ex-Israeli" and "ex-Jew" — and what it cost him ✔️The difference between Zionism and Judaism — and why that distinction matters for understanding the current war ✔️How Israel shifted from a secular to a theocratic state: from kibbutzim to Ben-Gvir ✔️Netanyahu's 1996 electoral discovery — and how it accelerated Israel's religious turn ✔️Rabbi Kook's philosophy and the roots of Greater Israel and the settler movement ✔️The role of AIPAC, political influence, and the "tail wagging the dog" debate ✔️Purim and Passover as religious frameworks shaping Israel's military conduct ✔️How the Nakba of 1948 was driven by secular ideology — and why Gaza is fundamentally different ✔️The distinction between anti-Zionist Orthodox Jews (Satmar, Neturei Karta) and the mainstream ✔️Jewish "chosenness" — as duty, or as supremacy? The political implications ✔️Why Atzmon argues Zionism has been defeated by Judaism — and what that means for peace ✔️The Iran war as the logical endpoint of Israel's religious-ideological transformation
🎙️ Guest: Gilad Atzmon — International Jazz Musician, Bestselling Author & Former IDF Conscript
📌 Views expressed are those of the guest and are presented for purposes of informed political and historical debate. Gilad Atzmon is a public intellectual whose work is widely discussed in academic and political contexts. Deep Dive Perspective presents multiple perspectives across our content. All assessments of Israeli state policy and ideology are the analytical opinions of the guest.
🔔 Subscribe for geopolitical analysis the mainstream won't touch | 👍 Like if this reframed how you understand the conflict | 💬 Share with someone who still thinks this is only about Zionism

entry.content:
Gilad Atzmon served in the IDF. He left Israel at 30. He now identifies as neither Israeli nor Jewish. And he argues that what's driving the war on Iran isn't Zionism — it's something older, deeper, and far more dangerous.

In this episode, Atzmon — international jazz musician, bestselling author and former IDF conscript — makes the case that Israel has undergone a fundamental transformation: from a secular Zionist project into a theocratic, religiously-driven state shaped by Judaic scripture, Rabbi Kook's philosophy, and a political culture of impunity. He traces this shift from the kibbutz era through Likud's 1977 victory, Netanyahu's 1996 electoral realignment, and into the religious nationalism of Ben-Gvir and Smotrich that now drives Israeli state policy — including the war on Iran.


🔍 In This Episode:

✔️Why Gilad Atzmon identifies as "ex-Israeli" and "ex-Jew" — and what it cost him
✔️The difference between Zionism and Judaism — and why that distinction matters for understanding the current war
✔️How Israel shifted from a secular to a theocratic state: from kibbutzim to Ben-Gvir
✔️Netanyahu's 1996 electoral discovery — and how it accelerated Israel's religious turn
✔️Rabbi Kook's philosophy and the roots of Greater Israel and the settler movement
✔️The role of AIPAC, political influence, and the "tail wagging the dog" debate
✔️Purim and Passover as religious frameworks shaping Israel's military conduct
✔️How the Nakba of 1948 was driven by secular ideology — and why Gaza is fundamentally different
✔️The distinction between anti-Zionist Orthodox Jews (Satmar, Neturei Karta) and the mainstream
✔️Jewish "chosenness" — as duty, or as supremacy? The political implications
✔️Why Atzmon argues Zionism has been defeated by Judaism — and what that means for peace
✔️The Iran war as the logical endpoint of Israel's religious-ideological transformation

🎙️ Guest: Gilad Atzmon — International Jazz Musician, Bestselling Author & Former IDF Conscript

entry.title: [DDP] What Trump Got Wrong About Iran — Dr. Bresheth Explains Why He Was Wrong
entry.summary:
"A whole civilisation will die tonight, never to be brought back again." Those are Donald Trump's own words on the night he declared Complete and Total Regime Change in Iran. Dr. Haim Bresheth — historian and geopolitical analyst — explains why that calculation is not just wrong, but catastrophically dangerous. From the destruction of F-35s over Iranian airspace to the collapse of Israel's air defence stockpiles, from Hezbollah's battlefield resilience to the terrifying logic of the Samson Option — Dr. Bresheth delivers one of the most forensic analyses of this conflict available anywhere. Trump told the Iranian people "God Bless you" while signing off on the bombing. Dr. Bresheth explains what that really means — and who is actually pulling the strings.
🔍 In This Episode:
✔️Trump's "regime change" declaration — what it actually means militarily and politically ✔️"A whole civilisation will die" — Dr. Bresheth's direct response to Trump's own words ✔️Why the US & Israel catastrophically miscalculated Iran's military capability ✔️Netanyahu's "forever war" strategy — why he cannot afford peace and faces jail without conflict ✔️How Trump is being managed by Netanyahu — and why every "off-ramp" has been closed ✔️Iran's F-35 kills, base destructions & the failure of conventional war doctrine ✔️Hezbollah's surprising battlefield resilience and the collapse of IDF morale ✔️Israel's air defence crisis: running out of interceptors at $3M each vs. $50 Iranian drones ✔️Why American soldiers will not fight and die for Israel's war ✔️Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz — charging for passage, selling oil in Chinese yuan ✔️De-dollarisation, BRICS and the financial war running parallel to the military one ✔️Gulf monarchies at risk: Kuwait, UAE and Qatar's existential vulnerability ✔️Iraq's PMUs entering the war and the Western strategy of regional chaos ✔️The Samson Option: what happens if Israel faces defeat — and the nuclear war risk ✔️China and Russia's dangerous inactivity — and what it means for the multipolar order ✔️The compromised IAEA, collapsed international institutions, and the role of independent media

entry.title: [DDP] Iran Has Been Preparing for This for Decades
entry.summary:
Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg & Dr. Mohammad Haidar expose how 47 years of Iranian preparation have left US strategy in ruins — as the Hormuz shutdown threatens global oil markets. 🔴 As the 74 iran war enters a critical new phase, three of the sharpest independent analysts in geopolitics break down what the mainstream refuses to report. Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg, and Dr. Mohammad Haidar make the case that the US and its allies fundamentally misread Iran's capabilities — and are now paying the price. With the 68 strait of hormuz under pressure, oil prices already at $100 a barrel, and Iran's underground missile stockpile estimated at 6,000–10,000 weapons, the picture that emerges is one of catastrophic Western miscalculation. This is the unfiltered analysis that corporate media won't air. In This Episode:
✅ Why Iran's 47-year preparation — 6,000 to 10,000 missiles in hardened underground facilities — has rendered the US "shock and awe" doctrine obsolete against a prepared adversary ✅ The devastating economics of a Strait of Hormuz closure: 20% of world oil supply, 80% of Gulf food imports, and a global insurance shock — Lloyd's premiums up 25–30% overnight ✅ Why the $7,000 Shahid drone vs. the $1.5 million US interceptor missile exposes a fundamental asymmetry that is bankrupting Washington's military posture in the region ✅ How the attempted decapitation of Iran's leadership created the opposite effect — uniting Iranian public opinion and producing a harder-line successor with deeper popular legitimacy ✅ Russia and China's calculated abstentions at the UN Security Council — and why this matters for the future of the multipolar order ✅ What Iran's conditions for ending the conflict reveal about the coming geopolitical reframing of the entire Middle East region
⏱️ CHAPTERS: 00:00 — Introduction: Iran's 47-Year Trap Sprung [TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Military Capabilities: 6,000 Missiles & a Strategy 47 Years in the Making [TIMESTAMP] — The Strait of Hormuz Shutdown: Global Oil, Food & Insurance in Crisis [TIMESTAMP] — The $7,000 Drone vs $1.5 Million Interceptor: Asymmetric Warfare Explained [TIMESTAMP] — US Decapitation Strategy Backfires: The Leadership Succession Effect [TIMESTAMP] — Russia & China at the UN: Abstention, Oil Revenue & the Multipolar Calculation [TIMESTAMP] — Iran's Peace Conditions: What the End of This War Looks Like [TIMESTAMP] — Closing Analysis: The Collapse of US Strategic Deterrence
🎙️ ABOUT VANESSA BEELEY: Vanessa Beeley is an independent investigative journalist and associate editor at 21st Century Wire, based in Beirut. Specialising in Middle East conflicts, Syria, and Western foreign policy propaganda, she is one of the most prominent voices in independent journalism on the region. 🎙️ ABOUT KIT KLARENBERG: Kit Klarenberg is an investigative journalist with The Grayzone, specialising in intelligence agencies, covert operations, and US foreign policy. His reporting has exposed Western information operations across the Middle East and Eastern Europe. 🎙️ ABOUT DR. MOHAMMED HAIDAR: Dr. Mohammad Haidar is a journalist and financial analyst specialising in Middle East economics, geopolitical risk, and the intersection of energy markets and imperial strategy.
🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE: Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. We cover geopolitics, war analysis, Middle East news, US foreign policy, and international relations with guests and experts from around the world. 📌 Subscribe for weekly deep dives: https://www.youtube.com/@DeepDivePerspective

entry.content:
Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg & Dr. Mohammad Haidar expose how 47 years of Iranian preparation have left US strategy in ruins — as the Hormuz shutdown threatens global oil markets.
🔴
As the 74 iran war enters a critical new phase, three of the sharpest independent analysts in geopolitics break down what the mainstream refuses to report. Vanessa Beeley, Kit Klarenberg, and Dr. Mohammad Haidar make the case that the US and its allies fundamentally misread Iran's capabilities — and are now paying the price. With the 68 strait of hormuz under pressure, oil prices already at $100 a barrel, and Iran's underground missile stockpile estimated at 6,000–10,000 weapons, the picture that emerges is one of catastrophic Western miscalculation. This is the unfiltered analysis that corporate media won't air.
In This Episode:

✅ Why Iran's 47-year preparation — 6,000 to 10,000 missiles in hardened underground facilities — has rendered the US "shock and awe" doctrine obsolete against a prepared adversary
✅ The devastating economics of a Strait of Hormuz closure: 20% of world oil supply, 80% of Gulf food imports, and a global insurance shock — Lloyd's premiums up 25–30% overnight
✅ Why the $7,000 Shahid drone vs. the $1.5 million US interceptor missile exposes a fundamental asymmetry that is bankrupting Washington's military posture in the region
✅ How the attempted decapitation of Iran's leadership created the opposite effect — uniting Iranian public opinion and producing a harder-line successor with deeper popular legitimacy
✅ Russia and China's calculated abstentions at the UN Security Council — and why this matters for the future of the multipolar order
✅ What Iran's conditions for ending the conflict reveal about the coming geopolitical reframing of the entire Middle East region

entry.title: [DDP] How Close Did Iran and America Really Come? | Bushra Shaikh & Dr. Helyeh Doutaghi
entry.summary:
Bushra Shaikh & Dr. Helyeh Doutaghi expose Trump's War on Iran as illegal aggression — why US miscalculations are now pushing Iran toward nuclear deterrence. 🔴 As Trump's threats to destroy Iranian civilian infrastructure dominate the iran war news cycle, the question of US intent has never been more urgent. Bushra and Dr. Helyeh — a geopolitical analyst and an academic based in Tehran — argue this is not regime change. It is balkanization: a deliberate strategy to fracture Iran and plunder its resources, driven by Israel and wrapped in Western propaganda designed to manufacture consent for illegal war. With the Israel-Iran war escalating and Iranian public opinion shifting dramatically toward nuclearization, this is the conversation the mainstream won't have.
In This Episode:
✅ Why Trump's attacks on gas plants, desalination facilities, and civilian energy grids constitute war crimes under international law — and what the US is actually targeting ✅ How the US war strategy has no coherent direction — and why Israel is driving the agenda behind the scenes ✅ How Western propaganda systematically dehumanises Iran to manufacture public consent for illegal military aggression ✅ Why Iran's fully decentralised infrastructure makes it impossible for US-Zionist forces to cripple the country — and why Washington is now in panic mode ✅ Why Iranian public support for nuclear weapons has surged from 30% to over 90% since the attacks began — and what this means for regional deterrence ✅ How October 7 "unmasked" Western liberal values — and what that reveals about the so-called rules-based international order
⏱️ CHAPTERS: 00:00 - Intro: The Escalating War in West Asia 01:10 - The Impact of US Attacks on Iranian Infrastructure 03:04 - Understanding the Legality of the Aggression 05:09 - The Incoherent Strategy: Why is the US Doing This? 06:59 - Deconstructing the Western Propaganda on Iran 11:59 - The Reality of Living in Iran vs. the Media Portrayal 15:20 - How Sanctions and Warfare Build Resistance 19:54 - Is Balkanization the Ultimate Goal for the US? 23:28 - Iran's Strategic Decentralization 36:33 - The Truth About Human Development in Iran 41:32 - The "Imperialist Boomerang": How Tactics Return Home 46:00 - Conclusion: The Unintended Consequences of Trump's Policies
🎙️ ABOUT Bushra Shaikh: Bushra Rache is a geopolitical analyst specialising in Middle East politics, US foreign policy, and Western imperialism. Known for her sharp, independent analysis of resistance movements and US strategic miscalculations, she is a regular contributor to Deep Dive Perspective. 🎙️ ABOUT DR. Helyeh Doutaghi: Dr. Helyeh is an academic and geopolitical analyst based in Tehran, specialising in international law, US-Iran relations, and anti-imperialist resistance theory. Expelled from Yale Law School for her vocal support of Palestine and Iran, she brings a rare and authoritative on-the-ground perspective from inside the country.
🌊 ABOUT DEEP DIVE PERSPECTIVE: Deep Dive Perspective explains how power, politics, and global systems really work — in a clear, honest, and unfiltered way. entry.title: [DDP] Could Iran Trigger the Next Global Shock? | Prof Marandi
entry.summary:
Are we on the brink of a global economic crisis? Discover how the escalating Iran vs The West dynamics will determine what happens next!
Welcome to the Deep Dive Perspective podcast. Today, Prof Marandi joins us to analyze the nuanced reality of Iran vs The West. As geopolitical tensions rise across the Middle East, many experts fear a global economic crisis is exactly what comes next. Prof Marandi breaks down the mainstream media narratives, complex regional responses, and the profound societal resilience shaping this historical moment. By examining the deep-rooted history of Iran vs The West, we explore how current foreign policy decisions might trigger a severe global economic crisis. Will international diplomacy finally prevail, or is a massive financial market shift what happens next? If you want the unfiltered truth behind the headlines, Prof Marandi delivers an unparalleled masterclass on international relations.
What do you think is the solution to this global tension? Share your thoughts in the comments below! Please hit the like button and subscribe to our channel to support independent journalism!
Guests Info: Professor Mohammad Marandi is a prominent political analyst, academic, and professor of North American Studies at the University of Tehran, known for his expert insights into international relations and Middle Eastern geopolitics.
🔔 SUBSCRIBE for more uncensored analysis that connects the dots the establishment wants to hide: https://www.youtube.com/@UCROsxSizsKbUzdARaocKDRQ
